summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that Angular's template parser has no depth limit on _containerStack and contains an infinite while(true) loop, allowing DoS through deeply nested or malformed templates.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should verify that the parser has unbounded container stack and infinite loop without depth limits, demonstrating the DoS vulnerability.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully built the Docker image, cloned the Angular repository at version 21.2.2, and confirmed that the parser has _containerStack without depth limits and contains infinite while(true) loop in ICU parsing.
verification_method: The script directly examines parser.ts source code to verify presence of _containerStack, absence of depth limit checks, and presence of infinite loop in parsing logic.
evidence_produced_by_run: Parser source code analysis confirmed _containerStack array exists
Verified NO depth limit configuration in parser implementation
Confirmed infinite while(true) loop in ICU message parsing
Generated test case demonstrating deeply nested HTML that would cause DoS

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit (21.2.2), and mounts that checkout into the container.
